data.title: Eli Lilly in pact with Schrödinger, Revvity for AI-driven drug discovery
data.description:
Schrödinger (SDGR) and Revvity (RVTY) on Friday announced collaborations with Eli Lilly (LLY) to integrate their respective drug development software offerings with the Indiana-based pharma giant’s TuneLab AI-based drug discovery platform.

According to Schrödinger (SDGR), its enterprise informatics platform, LiveDesign, will become a priority interface for biotech firms accessing LLY’s TuneLab, which leverages AI capabilities to accelerate drug discovery.

The New York-based company said that while a third party will host TuneLab on its platform, LLY and its partner firms can keep their proprietary data secure thanks to a privacy-focused method called federated learning.

“We are pleased to be partnering with Lilly to expand the use of digital drug design methods and ultimately drive greater impact for patients,” added Karen Akinsanya, SDGR’s president of R&D, therapeutics, and chief strategy officer, partnerships.

Meanwhile, Revvity (RVTY) said that Lilly TuneLab will be made available through its Revvity Signals platform, allowing the biotech community to benefit from Lilly’s advanced predictive models.

Additionally, the companies will jointly provide funding to selected biotech participants, allowing them to access Schrödinger’s (SDGR) Signals One, Signals Xynthetica, and other software offerings, as well as modeling credits.

Update 9:15 AM ET: Adds Lilly- Revvity partnership

data.title: TerraVest acquires KBK Industries for $90M
data.description:
• TerraVest Industries (TVK:CA) acquired KBK Industries, a Texas-based manufacturer of fiberglass and steel storage tanks, for US$90M in cash, subject to a post-closing working capital adjustment.

  • The purchase price represents ~5.6x KBK’s trailing twelve-month EBITDA.

  • KBK operates manufacturing facilities in Kansas and serves C-Store, Agricultural, Chemical, Infrastructure, and Energy markets across the U.S.

  • The acquisition complements TerraVest’s existing fiberglass tank operations in Canada and the U.S., including its Composite USA business acquired in January 2025.

  • TerraVest expects to enhance KBK’s sales channels by leveraging its established relationships in the C-Store market.

  • The deal adds geographic diversification and helps mitigate tariff exposure across TerraVest’s platform.

  • TerraVest funded the acquisition using its existing credit facility.

data.title: Semiconductors in focus as Mizuho downgrades Qualcomm, changes price targets
data.description:
Semiconductor stocks were in focus on Friday as Mizuho downgraded Qualcomm (QCOM) and tweaked its price targets on a host of other companies, including Micron (MU) and Western Digital (WDC). 

The firm cut its rating on Qualcomm to Neutral from Outperform and cut its price target to $175 from $200, noting it has “handset headwinds,” as it is expected to lose share in Apple (AAPL) devices this year and next. Qualcomm shares fell 1.4% in premarket trading.

Mizuho is positive on most of the rest of the sector, at least in the first-half of the year, as the artificial intelligence spending trade is set to continue. “We see potential for a strong 1H26E as AI spend, [wafer fab equipment] and memory cycles all expected to remain strong, powering tier1/2 CSP, enterprise/sovereign AI server ramps,” Mizuho analysts wrote in a note to clients. “Into 2H26E, we see potential challenges with 1) higher volatility around new product launches/ramps with Rubin/ MI400, 2) US midterm elections (11/3/2026), and 3) slower Fed rate cuts.”

The firm's top picks for 2026 are Nvidia (NVDA), Lumentum (LITE) and Broadcom (AVGO), and it also likes Microchip (MCHP) and Lam Research (LRCX) as “key outperformers.”

In addition, Mizuho also changed its price targets on several stocks. They are as follows:

  • Applied Materials (AMAT) was upped to $275 from $245.
  • Lam Research was raised to $220 from $200.
  • Lumentum was raised to $455 from $325.
  • MKS Inc. (MKSI) was raised to $215 from $195.
  • Micron (MU) was raised to $390 from $290.
  • Nvidia was raised to $275 from $245.
  • Onsemi (ON) was raised to $72 from $62.
  • Sandisk (SNDK) was raised to $410 from $250.
  • Seagate (STX) was raised to $370 from $290.
  • Super Micron (SMCI) was lowered to $31 from $45.
  • Western Digital (WDC) was raised to $240 from $180.
  • Qorvo (QRVO) was cut to $85 from $93. 
  • Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) was cut to $65 from $73.

data.title: Paranovus Entertainment Technology regains Nasdaq compliance; shares up
data.description:
• Paranovus Entertainment Technology Limited (PAVS) regained compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement on January 6, 2026.

  • The previously scheduled Nasdaq Hearings Panel meeting on January 29, 2026 was canceled and the matter closed.

  • PAVS shares up 14.6% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Pfizer inks licensing deal with Madrigal for MASH candidate
data.description:
Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL) announced on Friday an exclusive global license agreement with Pfizer (PFE) for ervogastat, an experimental therapy that could enhance the clinical effects of Rezdiffra, MDGL’s FDA-approved treatment for a liver disease called metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis.

Per the deal terms, Madrigal (MDGL) has obtained an exclusive global license to develop, manufacture, and commercialize ervogastat and two other early-stage MASH assets by paying $50M upfront to Pfizer (PFE), which will be reflected in the company's Q4 2025 financials.

Additionally, the New York-based pharma giant is eligible to receive certain milestone payments and royalties on net sales if products are commercialized.

Ervogastat, a liver-directed oral therapy, has a mechanism of action that could generate additional clinical benefits when combined with Rezdiffra, Madrigal (MDGL) said, noting that a drug-to-drug interaction study combining the drugs is expected to start this year.

“Because Rezdiffra and ervogastat act on distinct yet complementary pathways that drive liver fat accumulation, this combination has the potential to produce additive antisteatotic and antifibrotic efficacy,” said Professor Quentin Anstee, who has led a prior Phase 2 study of ervogastat.

data.title: January Fed rate cut bets dip after latest labor report
data.description:
Traders revised their rate cut bets for January after the latest labor report came in roughly in line, while the unemployment rate dipped to 4.4%.

After the latest job report, the chances of a 25 basis point rate cut stand at 3.9%, as compared to 11.1% a day ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

U.S. nonfarm payrolls grew by 50K in December, basically in line with the +55K consensus and slightly slower than the +56K in November (revised from +64K), according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Friday.

The next Federal Reserve policy decision is slated to be announced on January 28. 

In the last quarter of 2025, the Fed implemented three consecutive rate reductions, bringing down the central bank's policy rate by 75 basis points to a 3.50%-3.75% target range. But Fed officials expect that pace to slow markedly this year, with the median projection calling for just one quarter-point cut.

Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran's forecast calls for 1.5 percentage points of cuts to the federal funds rate target range, he said in a broadcast interview on Thursday. 

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Stocks to watch on Friday: TSM, GM, BABA, JNJ
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday as the key jobs report came largely as expected. Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

TSMC (TSM) rose 0.5% in premarket trading after reporting December 2025 revenue of approximately NT$335 billion on a consolidated basis. The figure represents a 2.5% decline from November but a 20.4% increase compared to the same month a year earlier. The chipmaker serves as a key supplier to major technology companies, including Apple, Nvidia, and AMD.

General Motors (GM) fell 2.4% before the opening bell after announcing it will take an additional $7.1 billion charge tied to its decision to scale back electric vehicle production and Chinese operations. The charge comes on top of the $1.6 billion the automaker previously recorded in the three months ended September 30.

Alibaba (BABA) slipped 2.51% in premarket trade despite its Hong Kong-listed shares rallying as much as 3.1%—marking their strongest intraday performance since late 2024. The surge was triggered by a Bloomberg report stating that Beijing is preparing to approve the import of Nvidia’s H200 AI chips for commercial use as early as this quarter.

Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) edged down 0.3% in premarket trading after entering an agreement with the Trump administration to reduce drug prices for millions of U.S. patients. The deal exempts the company’s products from U.S. tariffs, while Johnson & Johnson plans to build two new U.S. facilities in North Carolina and Pennsylvania as part of its $55 billion domestic investment in manufacturing and innovation.

data.title: Evolus sees Q4 net revenue between $88.6M and $90.6M
data.description:
• Evolus (EOLS) on Friday posted preliminary unaudited net revenue of $88.6M to $90.6M for Q4, representing 12% to 15% growth over the prior year.
  • The company reaffirmed positive Non-GAAP operating income of $5M to $7M for Q4 2025.
  • Evolus expects net revenue of $295.5M to $297.5M for the FY25, representing 11% to 12% growth over the prior year.
  • As of December 31, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents of $53 million compared to $43.5 million on September 30, 2025.
  • Consensus for Q4 and FY25 revenues are $90.91M and $297.47M.
  • Total net revenues for 2026 are projected to be between $327 million and $337 million, which represents 11% to 13% growth from preliminary unaudited 2025 results.
  • Evolysse and Estyme injectable HA gels are anticipated to contribute 10% to 12% of total revenue for the full-year 2026, it said.
  • The company expects to achieve sustainable annual profitability1 beginning in 2026.
  • For 2028, net revenue is expected between $450 million and $500 million, representing a three-year CAGR of 15% to 19%, and non-GAAP operating income margins of 13% to 15% for 2028.
  • EOLS +3.65% premarket to $5.95.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Aptevo Therapeutics jumps after securing $60 million equity line of credit
data.description:
• Aptevo Therapeutics (APVO) has entered into a $60 million equity line of credit agreement with Yorkville Advisors Global.
  • The facility provides Aptevo with access to capital, allowing the company to raise funds incrementally, at its discretion, and under market-based conditions. Proceeds from the financing will be used to support ongoing clinical development, advance preclinical programs, and fund general corporate purposes.
  • APVO +9.02% premarket to $10.03.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bitmine Immersion appoints Young Kim as CFO and COO
data.description:
Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) on Friday announced the appointment of Young Kim as CFO and COO, effective immediately.

In addition, Kim has been appointed a board member. Kim will report to Board Chairman Tom Lee.

From 2021 to 2025, Kim served as a partner and senior portfolio manager at Axiom Investors.

Bitmine is set to hold its annual meeting at the Wynn Las Vegas on January 15, 2026.

Shares were +0.03% pre-market to $31.29.

In November, BitMine Immersion Technologies had announced the appointment of Chi Tsang as CEO and a board member.
